Assignment week 9

Nora

2026-05-12

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(GenomicRanges)
  library(epiwraps)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(rGREAT) # Gene Ontology enrichment among genomic regions
})
## Warning: package 'S4Vectors' was built under R version 4.5.3

Part1

Download and decompress the following archive: https://ethz-ins.org/content/clustering_assignment.zip This contains the bigwig files and peaks (bed) files for three TFs of the CREB family (all restricted to chr1; aligned against the hg38 genome)

downloading data

options(timeout = 6000)
download.file("https://ethz-ins.org/content/clustering_assignment.zip", "clustering_assignment.zip")
unzip("clustering_assignment.zip")
list.files()

Prepare the regions and the tracks

tracks <- list.files(pattern="bw$")

### To get a clearer picture, focus on high-confidence peaks from each factor to define the universe of regions
peaks <- list.files(pattern="bed$")
# we first import the peaks
peaks <- lapply(peaks, rtracklayer::import.bed)
# we'll focus on the high-quality peaks
peaks <- lapply(peaks, FUN=function(x) x[x$score>800])
# we get the union of non-redundant regions
regions <- reduce(unlist(GRangesList(peaks)))

Part 2 Clustering and visualization

Use clustering and visualization to illustrate the relationship between the binding of the different proteins

ese <- signal2Matrix(tracks, regions, extend=2000)
## Reading Creb1.bw
## Reading Creb3.bw
## Reading Creb3L1.bw
set.seed(123)  # to ensure that it gives the same results everytime

# cluster version 1
cl <- clusterSignalMatrices(ese, k=4)
##   ~66% of the variance explained by clusters
rowData(ese)$cluster <- cl # put cluster as rowData for later maipulation of the object
head(rowData(ese))
## DataFrame with 6 rows and 1 column
##                     cluster
##                    <factor>
## chr1:778487-779069        4
## chr1:904436-904985        1
## chr1:941785-942096        1
## chr1:943126-943501        1
## chr1:959088-959463        1
## chr1:961148-961904        1
# plotting heatmap with defined colours
mycolors <- c("1"="red", "2"="blue", "3"="darkgreen", "4"="black", "5"="purple")
plotEnrichedHeatmaps(ese, row_split=rowData(ese)$cluster, mean_color=mycolors, colors=c("white","darkred"), use_raster = FALSE)

# Clustering using relative signal:
set.seed(123)
cl <- clusterSignalMatrices(ese, k=5, scaleRows = TRUE)
##   ~90% of the variance explained by clusters
rowData(ese)$cluster <- cl # put cluster as rowData for later maipulation of the object
d <- meltSignals(ese, splitBy=cl)
ggplot(d, aes(position, mean, colour=sample)) + geom_line() + facet_wrap(~split)

plotEnrichedHeatmaps(ese, row_split = cl, scale_rows = "global", mean_color=mycolors, use_raster = FALSE)

Part 3 Enrichement analysis

Use enrichment analysis (either GO or motif) on at least one of the clusters Write a paragraph describing your results Save your assignment in a R markdown named assignment.Rmd, render it, and push the html file to this folder in your github repository

Find what’s enriched in cluster 2 with respect to the clusters:

# splitting the regions by cluster:
split_regions <- split(rowRanges(ese), rowData(ese)$cluster)
lengths(split_regions)
##   1   2   3   4   5 
## 275 369 639 251 735
res <- great(split_regions[["2"]], gene_sets="GO:BP", tss_source="hg38", 
             background=regions, cores=2) # background is set to regions -> cluster 2 is compared with the other clusters
## * TSS source: TxDb.
## * check whether TxDb package 'TxDb.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38.knownGene' is installed.
## * gene ID type in the extended TSS is 'Entrez Gene ID'.
## * restrict chromosomes to 'chr1, chr2, chr3, chr4, chr5, chr6, chr7, chr8, chr9, chr10,
##     chr11, chr12, chr13, chr14, chr15, chr16, chr17, chr18, chr19, chr20, chr21, chr22, chrX,
##     chrY, chrM'.
## * 18193/35161 protein-coding genes left.
## * update seqinfo to the selected chromosomes.
## * TSS extension mode is 'basalPlusExt'.
## * construct the basal domains by extending 5000bp to upstream and 1000bp to downsteram of TSS.
## * calculate distances to neighbour regions.
## * extend to both sides until reaching the neighbour genes or to the maximal extension.
## * use GO:BP ontology with 15046 gene sets (source: org.Hs.eg.db).
## * check gene ID type in `gene_sets` and in `extended_tss`.
## * use self-defined background regions.
## * remove excluded regions from background.
## * overlap `gr` to background regions (based on midpoint).
## * in total 369 `gr`.
## * overlap extended TSS to background regions.
## * check which genes are in the gene sets.
## * only take gene sets with size >= 5.
## * in total 2479 gene sets.
## * overlap `gr` to every extended TSS.
## * perform binomial test for each biological term.
bp <- getEnrichmentTables(res)

head(bp)
##           id                                              description
## 1 GO:0048646 anatomical structure formation involved in morphogenesis
## 2 GO:0035239                                       tube morphogenesis
## 3 GO:0072359                           circulatory system development
## 4 GO:0035295                                         tube development
## 5 GO:0007589                                     body fluid secretion
## 6 GO:0009791                               post-embryonic development
##   genome_fraction observed_region_hits fold_enrichment      p_value  p_adjust
## 1     0.142301751                   78        1.485450 0.0002035889 0.2950004
## 2     0.114106272                   63        1.496252 0.0007802752 0.4336230
## 3     0.135468423                   72        1.440350 0.0008977702 0.4336230
## 4     0.136599015                   70        1.388750 0.0027247559 0.6666767
## 5     0.009444712                   10        2.869359 0.0030534430 0.6666767
## 6     0.008029694                    9        3.037506 0.0033595331 0.6666767
##   mean_tss_dist observed_gene_hits gene_set_size fold_enrichment_hyper
## 1         58781                 52           100              1.697358
## 2         45539                 40            73              1.788576
## 3         51605                 48            86              1.821852
## 4         49345                 46            90              1.668344
## 5         78375                  4             6              2.176101
## 6          2307                  7             9              2.538784
##   p_value_hyper p_adjust_hyper
## 1  3.008237e-06   0.0021794674
## 2  8.909555e-06   0.0043033149
## 3  4.815481e-07   0.0006977632
## 4  2.177006e-05   0.0078862031
## 5  7.519134e-02   0.2690179130
## 6  4.767492e-03   0.1409815482

We plot the top Biological Processes:

ggplot(head(bp,15), aes(fold_enrichment, reorder(description, p_adjust), 
                        size=observed_region_hits, color=-log10(p_adjust))) + 
  geom_point() + scale_color_viridis_c()

The background for calculating the enrichment was set to the regions. So we can check the enrichment of GO terms of cluster 2 compared to the remaining 4 clusters. From the plot before we know that in cluster 2 there’s a high level of Creb1 binding but low binding of the other two proteins (Creb3 & Creb3L1). Multiple GO terms are related to the nervous system, which is not surprising as Creb1 is known to be important for learning/memory and neural function. In addition there are 3 GO terms with a high fold enrichment but low observed region hits and therefore not surprising also a low p-value: myofibril assembly, endocrine system development, protein localization to cell surface. The GO term “central neervous system neuron differentiation” shows a high fold-enrichment and at the same time also a low p-value (eventhough the observed region hits is not high.) So in a next step I would be interesting to take a closer look at that GO-term.
